response:
# Confirmation Delete Modal Accessibility Testing Plan

> **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after test plan implementation.
> **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA
> **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only users, low vision users, users relying on predictable focus order
> **Assistive technologies:** NVDA on Windows, JAWS on Windows, VoiceOver on macOS, keyboard-only navigation

**Feature:** Confirmation modal dialog for deleting a project, requiring the user to type the project name before enabling destructive deletion.  
**Risk Level:** Medium  
**Component/Page Type:** Modal Dialog with confirmation form input  
**Plan path:** `docs/a11y-plans/2026-06-12-delete-project-modal-a11y-test-plan.md`

---

## Scope & Context

This plan covers accessibility testing for an existing React/TypeScript confirmation modal used in a project management SaaS application.

The modal opens from a **Delete Project** trigger button. It contains:

- Dialog title: `Delete Project: {name}`
- Description explaining the destructive action
- Text input requiring exact project name confirmation
- Disabled/enabled destructive `Delete` button
- `Cancel` button
- Escape key close behavior
- Backdrop click close behavior
- Focus restoration to the original trigger
- Background content made unavailable with `inert`

The core accessibility risk is not whether the modal has a role. The risk is the full focus lifecycle: **open → announce → trap focus → prevent background access → update disabled state → close → restore focus**. A modal that passes axe but leaks focus or fails restoration is still broken for keyboard and screen reader users.

This plan does **not** claim the implementation is accessible. It defines what QA must verify before PR merge.

---

## Standards Grounding

Primary interaction pattern:

- WAI-ARIA APG Modal Dialog Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialogmodal/

Relevant WCAG 2.2 criteria:

- **1.3.1 Info and Relationships:** dialog title, description, label, and error/help relationships are programmatically determinable.
- **2.1.1 Keyboard:** all modal actions work with keyboard.
- **2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap:** focus is trapped only while modal is open, with valid exits through Cancel, Escape, backdrop, or successful action.
- **2.4.3 Focus Order:** focus moves predictably on open and returns to the trigger on close.
- **2.4.7 Focus Visible:** focused controls have visible indicators.
- **2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured:** focused modal controls are not hidden behind sticky UI or viewport clipping.
- **2.5.8 Target Size Minimum:** modal buttons and close/cancel targets meet minimum hit area.
- **3.3.2 Labels or Instructions:** confirmation input has clear instructions.
- **4.1.2 Name, Role, Value:** dialog role, accessible name, description, input label, and button disabled state are exposed.
- **4.1.3 Status Messages:** dynamic state changes that matter to users are announced or otherwise discoverable.

Important constraint: `inert` is the primary background isolation mechanism. `aria-modal="true"` may be tested as a compatibility fallback, but this plan does **not** require it as the sole background-blocking mechanism.

---

## Semantic Structure Plan

Expected modal structure:

```text
Delete Project trigger button
main application container
  normal page content

modal layer, when open
  backdrop
  dialog container
    title: Delete Project: {name}
    description paragraph
    form or grouped confirmation controls
      visible label for confirmation input
      confirmation text input
      Delete button
      Cancel button
```

Required semantic relationships:

| Element | Requirement | WCAG |
|---|---|---|
| Dialog container | Has `role="dialog"` and accessible name via `aria-labelledby` | 4.1.2 |
| Dialog title | Visible title text referenced by dialog | 1.3.1, 4.1.2 |
| Description | Visible explanatory text referenced by `aria-describedby` | 1.3.1, 4.1.2 |
| Confirmation input | Has visible label, not title-only naming | 1.3.1, 3.3.2 |
| Delete button | Programmatic disabled state while unavailable | 4.1.2 |
| Background container | Receives `inert` while modal is open | 2.1.1, 2.4.3 |
| Trigger button | Regains focus after any cancel/close path | 2.4.3 |

---

## Interaction Pattern Design

### Interactive Elements Table

| Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA / DOM Attributes To Verify | WCAG Citation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Delete Project trigger | Button | Tab to focus, Enter/Space opens modal | Native button preferred; accessible name includes delete action | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 |
| Modal dialog | APG Modal Dialog | On open, focus moves into dialog; Tab remains inside; Escape closes | `role="dialog"`, `aria-labelledby`, `aria-describedby`; `aria-modal` optional compatibility fallback | 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.4.3, 4.1.2 |
| Confirmation input | Native text input | Tab focus, text entry, standard editing keys | Visible `<label>` association; optional hint via `aria-describedby` | 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 3.3.2 |
| Delete button | Button | Tab focus; Enter/Space activates only when enabled | Native `disabled` preferred; if custom, `aria-disabled` plus blocked activation | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 |
| Cancel button | Button | Tab focus; Enter/Space closes modal | Native button; accessible name “Cancel” | 2.1.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.2 |
| Backdrop | Modal dismissal affordance | Mouse/pointer click outside dialog closes; keyboard users use Escape/Cancel | Backdrop itself should not be in tab order | 2.1.1, 2.5.8 |
| Escape key | APG Modal Dialog | Pressing Escape closes modal and restores focus | No ARIA attribute; behavior requirement | 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.4.3 |

### Expected Screen Reader Announcement

When the modal opens and focus moves to the dialog container, NVDA should announce enough information for the user to understand:

```text
Delete Project: {name}
dialog
[description text]
```

Order varies by browser/screen reader pair. The acceptance criterion is not exact wording. QA should verify that the announcement includes:

- Dialog role or equivalent modal context
- Visible title text: `Delete Project: {name}`
- Description text explaining the destructive action
- Focus is inside the modal, not left on the background trigger

For JAWS and VoiceOver, QA should verify equivalent name, role, and description exposure. Do not require byte-for-byte parity across screen readers.

---

## Focus Management Plan

### Expected Focus Lifecycle

1. User tabs to `Delete Project` trigger.
2. User presses Enter or Space.
3. Modal opens.
4. Background app container receives `inert`.
5. Focus moves into the dialog. Current implementation says focus moves to the dialog container. This is acceptable only if the dialog container is programmatically focusable and the announcement includes title and description.
6. Tab moves through modal controls only.
7. Shift+Tab moves backward through modal controls only.
8. Escape, Cancel, or backdrop click closes the modal.
9. Focus returns to the original `Delete Project` trigger.

### Focus Trap Test Script

QA should execute this exact keyboard sequence:

1. Focus the `Delete Project` trigger using Tab.
2. Press Enter.
3. Verify modal is visible.
4. Verify active element is the dialog container or first intended modal focus target.
5. Press Tab repeatedly through all focusable modal controls:
   - Confirmation input
   - Delete button, if focusable while disabled; otherwise skip if native disabled
   - Cancel button
   - Any close button, if present
6. Continue pressing Tab once past the last focusable modal control.
7. Verify focus wraps to the first focusable modal control inside the modal.
8. Press Shift+Tab from the first focusable control.
9. Verify focus wraps to the last focusable modal control inside the modal.
10. Verify no background links, buttons, nav items, or project controls receive focus while modal is open.

Native disabled buttons are removed from tab order. If the `Delete` button uses native `disabled`, QA should not expect to tab to it while disabled. If the implementation uses `aria-disabled="true"` instead, it may remain focusable, but activation must be blocked and the disabled state must be announced.

---

## State Communication Design

### State Communication Table

| State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA / DOM Attribute | WCAG Citation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Modal closed | No dialog visible; background interactive | Dialog absent or hidden; background not inert | No stale dialog in accessibility tree | 2.4.3, 4.1.2 |
| Modal open | Dialog and backdrop visible | Dialog role exposed; background isolated | `role="dialog"`, `aria-labelledby`, `aria-describedby`, background `inert` | 2.1.2, 4.1.2 |
| Background unavailable | Background visually dimmed | Background cannot receive focus or SR navigation | `inert` on app/background container | 2.1.1, 2.4.3 |
| Input empty/mismatch | Delete button visually disabled | Delete action unavailable | Native `disabled` or `aria-disabled="true"` with blocked activation | 4.1.2 |
| Input matches project name | Delete button visually enabled | Delete action available | Remove `disabled` or set `aria-disabled="false"` | 4.1.2 |
| Escape close | Modal disappears | Focus restored to trigger | DOM focus on original trigger | 2.1.1, 2.4.3 |
| Cancel close | Modal disappears | Focus restored to trigger | DOM focus on original trigger | 2.1.1, 2.4.3 |
| Backdrop close | Modal disappears | Focus restored to trigger | DOM focus on original trigger | 2.4.3, 2.5.8 |

---

## Automated Testing Setup

Use Playwright with axe-core in CI.

Recommended test layers:

1. **Static or component-level checks**
   - Validate modal attributes exist in rendered output.
   - Verify `aria-labelledby` and `aria-describedby` reference real element IDs.
   - Verify input has a visible associated label.

2. **Runtime axe scan**
   - Run axe when modal is closed.
   - Run axe when modal is open.
   - Run axe with input mismatch.
   - Run axe with input matching project name and Delete enabled.

3. **Keyboard behavior tests**
   - Real Playwright keyboard events for Tab, Shift+Tab, Escape, Enter, and Space.
   - Do not substitute click-only tests for keyboard tests.

4. **DOM verification**
   - Confirm `inert` lands on the correct background container.
   - Confirm the modal itself is not inside an inert subtree.
   - Confirm aria references resolve to visible text.

### Playwright Assertions To Include

The QA engineer should assert:

- Dialog exists after trigger activation.
- Dialog has `role="dialog"`.
- Dialog has `aria-labelledby` pointing to the visible title.
- Dialog has `aria-describedby` pointing to the visible description.
- Background app container has `inert` while modal is open.
- Background app container does not have `inert` after modal closes.
- Focus remains within dialog during Tab/Shift+Tab cycling.
- Escape closes modal.
- Cancel closes modal.
- Backdrop click closes modal.
- Focus returns to the trigger for all close paths.
- Delete button is unavailable until exact project name match.
- Delete button becomes available after exact project name match.
- No axe violations exist in each modal state.

---

## Manual Screen Reader Testing

Because the QA engineer has basic accessibility knowledge, keep manual SR testing focused and repeatable.

### NVDA Smoke Test

Environment: Windows, Chrome or Firefox, NVDA.

Steps:

1. Navigate to the `Delete Project` trigger with Tab.
2. Press Enter.
3. Listen to the opening announcement.
4. Confirm NVDA announces:
   - The modal title, `Delete Project: {name}`
   - The role/context, such as “dialog”
   - The description text or enough descriptive context
5. Press Tab through the modal controls.
6. Confirm each control announces name, role, and state:
   - Confirmation input: label and edit field
   - Delete button: unavailable/disabled until match
   - Cancel button: button
7. Type a non-matching value.
8. Confirm Delete remains disabled/unavailable.
9. Type the exact project name.
10. Confirm Delete becomes available. If the state change is not automatically announced, it must be discoverable when focus reaches the button.
11. Press Escape.
12. Confirm focus returns to `Delete Project` trigger.

### JAWS and VoiceOver

Run the same scenario. Acceptance is equivalent exposure, not identical wording.

Minimum pass condition:

- User can identify that a modal dialog opened.
- User hears the title.
- User hears or can reach the description.
- User can operate input and buttons.
- User cannot reach background controls while modal is open.
- User returns to the trigger after close.

---

## Visual Regression Testing

Capture snapshots for these states:

1. Modal closed.
2. Modal open with empty confirmation input.
3. Modal open with mismatched input.
4. Modal open with exact-match input and Delete enabled.
5. Keyboard focus on:
   - Dialog container, if initial focus target
   - Confirmation input
   - Cancel button
   - Delete button when enabled
6. Modal at 200% zoom.
7. Modal at narrow viewport equivalent to 320 CSS px width.
8. Modal with high contrast / forced colors mode if supported by test environment.
9. Modal with reduced motion enabled, if opening/closing animation exists.

Visual acceptance criteria:

- Focus indicator is visible and not clipped.
- Dialog content remains readable at 200% zoom.
- No horizontal scrolling is required at narrow viewport unless content genuinely cannot reflow.
- Disabled and enabled Delete button states are distinguishable by more than color, such as text, opacity plus disabled semantics, or icon/state styling.
- Backdrop does not obscure focused modal content.
- Buttons meet at least 24x24 CSS px target size, with 44x44 preferred.

---

## Test Prioritization And Execution Order

1. **Blocker checks before merge**
   - axe-core scan with modal open and closed
   - DOM verification for role, label references, description references
   - `inert` on background while open
   - focus trap
   - Escape close and focus restoration
   - Cancel close and focus restoration

2. **High-priority interaction checks**
   - backdrop click close and focus restoration
   - Delete button disabled/enabled state
   - exact project name matching behavior
   - no background focus leakage

3. **Screen reader smoke checks**
   - NVDA announcement on open
   - NVDA disabled/enabled Delete state
   - JAWS and VoiceOver sanity checks before release candidate

4. **Visual checks**
   - focus visibility
   - zoom/reflow
   - high contrast
   - reduced motion

This order is intentional. Focus lifecycle failures are more damaging than cosmetic drift and are often missed by axe.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

The modal passes accessibility testing only if all of the following are true:

- The modal follows APG Modal Dialog behavior.
- Opening the modal moves focus into the dialog.
- The dialog exposes name, role, and description.
- Background content is inert while the modal is open.
- Keyboard focus cannot escape to background content.
- Escape closes the modal and restores focus to the trigger.
- Cancel closes the modal and restores focus to the trigger.
- Backdrop click closes the modal and restores focus to the trigger.
- Confirmation input has a visible accessible label.
- Delete action is unavailable until the exact project name is entered.
- Disabled/enabled Delete state is programmatically exposed.
- All modal states pass axe-core in Playwright.
- Focus indicators are visible and not obscured.
- Manual NVDA smoke test confirms dialog announcement and usable interaction.
- No close path leaves focus lost on `body`, removed DOM nodes, or background content.

---

## Test Cases

### Test 1: Modal Opens With Correct Semantics

Steps:

1. Navigate to Delete Project trigger.
2. Activate with Enter.
3. Verify dialog appears.

Assertions:

- `role="dialog"` is present.
- `aria-labelledby` references the visible title.
- `aria-describedby` references the visible description.
- Dialog title includes the project name.
- Background container has `inert`.
- axe-core reports no violations.

WCAG: 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 4.1.2.

### Test 2: Focus Trap

Steps:

1. Open modal.
2. Press Tab through all controls.
3. Press Tab once more after the last modal control.
4. Press Shift+Tab from the first modal control.

Assertions:

- Focus stays inside dialog.
- Tab wraps forward.
- Shift+Tab wraps backward.
- No background element receives focus.

WCAG: 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.4.3.

### Test 3: Escape Close And Focus Restoration

Steps:

1. Focus trigger.
2. Open modal.
3. Press Escape.

Assertions:

- Modal closes.
- Background `inert` is removed.
- Focus returns to Delete Project trigger.

WCAG: 2.1.1, 2.4.3.

### Test 4: Cancel Close And Focus Restoration

Steps:

1. Open modal.
2. Tab to Cancel.
3. Press Enter or Space.

Assertions:

- Modal closes.
- Background `inert` is removed.
- Focus returns to Delete Project trigger.

WCAG: 2.1.1, 2.4.3.

### Test 5: Backdrop Close And Focus Restoration

Steps:

1. Open modal.
2. Click outside the dialog on the backdrop.

Assertions:

- Modal closes.
- Background `inert` is removed.
- Focus returns to Delete Project trigger.
- Backdrop itself was not keyboard-focusable.

WCAG: 2.4.3, 2.5.8.

### Test 6: Delete Button Disabled/Enabled State

Steps:

1. Open modal.
2. Verify Delete button starts disabled/unavailable.
3. Type an incorrect project name.
4. Verify Delete remains disabled/unavailable.
5. Clear input.
6. Type exact project name.
7. Verify Delete becomes enabled/available.

Assertions:

- Disabled state is programmatically exposed with native `disabled` or correct `aria-disabled`.
- Enabled state updates programmatically.
- Disabled Delete cannot be activated.
- Enabled Delete can be activated with Enter/Space.

WCAG: 2.1.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2.

### Test 7: Screen Reader Opening Announcement

Steps:

1. Start NVDA.
2. Tab to trigger.
3. Press Enter.
4. Listen to announcement.

Assertions:

- Title is announced.
- Dialog role/context is announced.
- Description is announced or reachable immediately.
- Focus is inside modal.

WCAG: 1.3.1, 4.1.2.

---

## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints

| Checkpoint | After | Focus Areas |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Automated Playwright tests are written | Verify tests cover focus lifecycle, not only static ARIA |
| 2 | axe-core CI integration is added | Verify modal-open and modal-closed states are both scanned |
| 3 | Keyboard tests pass | Verify Tab/Shift+Tab trap, Escape, Cancel, and backdrop restoration |
| 4 | Screen reader smoke test completed | Verify announcement expectations are specific enough and results documented |
| 5 | Before PR merge | Verify `inert` is primary background isolation and no stale focus paths remain |

---

## Failure Modes This Plan Prevents

- Modal passes axe but allows Tab focus into the background.
- Escape closes the modal but focus lands on `body`.
- Cancel restores focus but backdrop click does not.
- `aria-labelledby` points to a missing or hidden element.
- Description exists visually but is not programmatically associated.
- Background visually dims but remains screen-reader reachable.
- Delete button looks disabled but remains activatable.
- Delete button state changes visually but not programmatically.
- QA verifies “modal announced” without checking title, role, and description.
- Visual snapshots miss focus indicators and zoom behavior.

---

## References

- WAI-ARIA APG Modal Dialog Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialogmodal/
- WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/
- WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/
- axe-core: https://github.com/dequelabs/axe-core
- Playwright Accessibility Testing: https://playwright.dev/
